name: Wat Phra Singh
description: Chiang Mai's most revered temple, housing the Phra Singh Buddha image. Established in 1345 AD, the Lanna-style architecture draws both devout pilgrims and cultural tourists.

name: Wat Chedi Luang
description: A 15th-century temple dominated by a partially ruined 60-metre chedi. The evening monk-chat programme makes it popular well into the night.

name: Wat Doi Suthep
description: Iconic mountaintop temple visible from the city below. The 309-step Naga staircase and panoramic terrace make it Chiang Mai's most photographed landmark.

name: Sunday Walking Street
description: Wualai Road transforms every Sunday into a kilometre-long market of crafts, street food, and live performance. Peak density rivals any market in Southeast Asia.

name: Nimmanhaemin Road
description: Chiang Mai's creative quarter lined with specialty-coffee cafés, art galleries, and boutiques. Strong draw for digital nomads and long-stay visitors.

name: Doi Inthanon National Park
description: Thailand's highest peak at 2,565 m. Twin royal pagodas, cloud-forest birding, and Mae Ya Waterfall make it the premier nature destination in Northern Thailand.

name: Night Bazaar
description: Sprawling nightly market on Chang Khlan Road offering souvenirs, handicrafts, street food, and cultural shows. Operating since the 1960s as a major tourist draw.

name: Tha Phae Gate
description: The eastern gate of Chiang Mai's old city moat, dating to 1296 AD. Central orientation point for old-city exploration and the focal node of Yi Peng lantern festival celebrations.

name: Suan Dok Temple
description: 14th-century temple housing a large collection of royal bone relics. White chedis against the sunset make it a favourite among photographers.

name: Warorot Market
description: Chiang Mai's oldest covered market, open since 1910. Three floors of fresh produce, northern Thai textiles, dried foods, and herbs attract locals and culinary tourists.

name: Chiang Mai Zoo
description: Hillside zoo home to giant pandas on loan from China, plus over 400 animal species. Cable car to Doi Suthep departs from the zoo grounds.

name: Chiang Mai Night Safari
description: Open-air wildlife park with electric tram tours after dark. Designed to offer close-range encounters with nocturnal animals in simulated natural habitats.

name: Wat Umong
description: Ancient forest temple founded in 1297 AD with underground meditation tunnels. A serene counterpoint to the busy old city; popular with meditation practitioners.

name: Royal Flora Ratchaphruek
description: Royal botanical garden developed for the 2006 International Horticultural Exposition. Features the Royal Pavilion and themed gardens representing all 77 Thai provinces.

name: Wat Doi Kham
description: Hilltop temple revered for its large golden Naga and a 17-metre standing Buddha with panoramic views over southern Chiang Mai. Frequented mainly by local devotees.

name: Maya Lifestyle Shopping Center
description: Contemporary lifestyle mall at the top of Nimman Road, anchored by international brands, a cinema multiplex, and a rooftop garden with mountain views.

name: Doi Pui Tribal Village
description: Hmong hill-tribe community on the slopes of Doi Suthep, showcasing traditional crafts, costumes, and agricultural practices. Often combined with Doi Suthep temple visits.

name: Mae Kampong Village
description: Charming mountain village celebrated for community-based eco-tourism, miang tea cultivation, and scenic waterfalls. A model of sustainable tourism development in Thailand.

name: Baan Tawai Crafts Village
description: Northern Thailand's premier wood-carving destination with hundreds of workshops producing teak furniture, lacquerware, and intricate decorative pieces exported worldwide.

name: San Kamphaeng Hot Springs
description: Geothermal mineral springs 35 km east of Chiang Mai. The main geyser reaches 90°C; visitors enjoy private mineral-water pools for therapeutic soaking.

name: Ang Kaew Reservoir (CMU)
name_en: Ang Kaew Reservoir — Chiang Mai University
description: Tranquil reservoir on the Chiang Mai University campus framed by forested hills. Popular morning jogging circuit and photography spot for mist over the water.

name: Central Festival Chiang Mai
description: Large retail complex adjacent to Chiang Mai International Airport, housing a department store, multiplex cinema, international food outlets, and a fresh market.

name: Wat Chiang Man
description: Chiang Mai's oldest temple, founded in 1296 AD by King Mengrai as a temporary royal residence during the city's construction. Houses two highly revered ancient Buddha images.

name: Chiang Mai City Arts and Cultural Centre
name_en: Chiang Mai City Arts & Cultural Centre
description: Housed in a 1920s colonial building, this museum presents Lanna history from the foundation of Chiang Mai through to modern times via interactive multimedia exhibits.
